title: How Helm stores releases and tracks revisions
description: Covers release state stored as Secrets/ConfigMaps in the namespace, revision history, and the deployed/superseded/failed/pending status model.
body: Use when reasoning about Helm state and rollbacks. Do not assume Helm tracks state in the cluster's live objects. Actions: know each release revision is stored as a Secret (default driver) in the release namespace, helm history reads those to show revisions and statuses (deployed, superseded, failed, pending-upgrade), and rollback creates a new revision reapplying an old manifest. Inspect with helm history and kubectl get secret -l owner=helm. Validation signals include the revision count matching history and a rollback adding a new deployed revision. Risk boundary: deleting release secrets corrupts history and is destructive.
